headline: Robinhood Broadens Beyond Trading With Venture Fund And Platinum Card
summary: Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD) has launched Robinhood Ventures Fund I, a closed end venture fund listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The fund is designed to give retail investors exposure to private companies that have typically been accessible mainly to institutional investors. Robinhood has also introduced a Platinum credit card with a reported annual fee of US$695, entering the high end credit card segment. Robinhood built its brand around low cost stock and options trading and...

headline: Nasdaq teams up with Kraken to expand tokenization infrastructure
summary: Nasdaq on Monday announced it will collaborate with Payward, parent company of cryptocurrency exchange Kraken, to develop ‌tokenization infrastructure, aiming to capitalize on the growing interest in ‌blockchain-based equities. Tokenization is the process of turning financial assets - such as bank deposits, stocks, ​bonds, funds and even real estate - into crypto assets that can be traded on blockchains, which are the ledgers underpinning digital assets.

headline: Corrections & Amplifications
summary: Vinay Prasad, the Food and Drug Administration’s vaccines chief, refused in February to consider a flu-shot application from  Moderna  before the FDA reversed course.  In some editions Saturday, a U.S. News article about Prasad’s plans to leave the agency incorrectly said the refusal was earlier this month.  A neurotoxin similar to Botox was used in a cosmetic procedure described in a U.S. News article on Tuesday about Planned Parenthood Mar Monte.
